 1585 + ## Session 72 (2026-04-16) — DRE Family Collapsed 21→13 + Steven's claude.ai Copy Delivered (Read-Only Session)
 1586 +
 1587 + **Session theme:** Take S71's theoretical 21-sequence DRE framework and collapse it to a data-backed sequence set by running live classification passes over the SF Leads universe. Five classif

 + ication passes + two filter passes on 76,471 rows (measured) produced 13 locked sequences covering 26,137 eligible leads (measured). Session ended at context-budget ceiling with Steven's claud

 + e.ai-revised copy file landed in repo but not yet diffed / integrated / built — Phase 2 deferred cleanly to S73.

 1588 +

 1589 + ### 1. Five classification passes produced the 13-sequence lock

 1590 +

 1591 + - Pass 1–5 iterated on SF Leads role + grade + substrategy classification. The S71 theoretical matrix (TC 9 × LQD 9 × LIB 2 × UNI 1 = 21 sequences) did not survive contact with the real data —

 + several cells had <50 leads and collapsed into residuals.

 1592 + - Final lock: **13 sequences** with eligible cohorts (all measured, after active-account and 90-day no-touch filters):

 1593 + 1. INT-Universal 867 (ship first as safety net)

 1594 + 2. TC-Universal-Residual 6,957

 1595 + 3. TC-MS 5,452

 1596 + 4. TC-HS 4,892

 1597 + 5. TC-Elem 4,941

 1598 + 6. TC-Virtual 441

 1599 + 7. TC-District 442

 1602 + 10. LQD-Universal 407

 1603 + 11. INT-Teacher 404

 1604 + 12. TC-Teacher 393

 1605 + 13. IT-ReEngage 182 (TC-IT + INT-IT merged)

 1606 + - TC substrategy dominates at **89% measured** of the DRE universe. Full derivation at `~/Desktop/DRE_Sequence_Family_Derivation_S72.md`.

 1607 + - Framework memory rewritten at `project_dre_family_framework.md` to reflect the locked 13 (replacing S71's 21).

 1608 +

 1609 + ### 2. Steven's claude.ai copy file landed — not yet integrated

 1610 +

 1612 + - Represents Steven's iteration pass on the S71 v2 base copy through claude.ai.

 1613 + - **Not diffed against S71 v2 base. Not integrated. Not validated against `feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md`.** Session 72 hit context-budget ceiling before Phase 2 work could start.

 1614 + - Phase 2 work = S73 first actions (listed in CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE).

 1615 +

 1616 + ### 3. Key finding that changes the add workflow

 1617 +

 1618 + - 99.83% measured of eligible DRE leads already exist in Outreach as prospects. S73 workflow is **`find_prospect_by_email` + verify state+tz + `add_prospect_to_sequence`** — NOT `create_prospe

 + ct`. Only 36 measured leads need creation.

 1619 + - Rule 17 is mostly verification-not-population for DRE.

 1620 +

 1626 + - `project_dre_cold_outreach_parked_cohort.md` (new — ~48k measured parked bought-list leads for future cold session)

 1627 + - **Reusable artifacts at /tmp (preserve across sessions):**

 1629 + - `sf_leads_dre_filters.py` + `sf_leads_dre_filters_part2.py` (filters)

 1630 + - `dre_outreach_touch_cache.json` (26,944 measured `touchedAt` entries — reuse to skip API calls)

 + gage appropriate. Reference: `project_dre_cold_outreach_parked_cohort.md`.

 Pivot: S73 DRE Phase 1. Per CLAUDE.md line 15, first actions are (1) load 6 memory files, (2) read docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md end-to-end, (3) extract shared base voice, (4) verify each of 13
 sequences against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , (5) enter plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers + INT-Universal build.

 Pivot: S73 DRE Phase 1. Per CLAUDE.md line 15, first actions are (1) load 6 memory files, (2) read docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md end-to-end, (3) extract shared base voice, (4) verify each of 13
 sequences against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , (5) enter plan-mode for th 3 filter helpers + INT-Universal build.

 Before I load 6 memory files and a 40KB copy file — quick context check. That's a substantial read ( ~25–35K tokens input). The current ctx: -- marker means the sidecar isn't feeding me a real number, so I
 can't self-gauge via the 40% rule. I want to do this right.

 Two paths — which do you want:

 (a) Full Phase 1 in this turn. I load all 6 memory files, read the full 40KB copy end-to-end, extract the shared voice, run the 13-sequence verification pass against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , and come
 back with: voice spec + pass/fail per sequence + entering plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers and INT-Universal. Likely burns significant ctx but is what the S73 first-actions block prescribes.

 (b) Staged. I start with the 6 memory files + skim the copy file's table-of-contents / sequence headers only, report what shape the file is actually in, then you decide whether to do the full voice extraction
 + verification in this turn or split. Lower ctx burn upfront, slower overall.

 My recommendation: (a) . The S73 actions were deliberately scoped by S72-me to fit in one session of focused work, and splitting the verification across turns risks losing the shared-voice context between
 sequences 1–7 and 8–13. But if you want to conserve budget, (b) is valid.

 7 thought for 24s)

 Searched for 3 patterns, read 5 files, listed 1 directory, recalled 3 memories (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Surveyed enough. Critical constraints found:

 1. create_sequence creates a new template for every step (outreach_client.py:1354-1371) — no path to reference an existing template_id . Step 2 = "template 43784" means I'd duplicate 43784's content into 13 new
 templates OR patch the code.

 2. VALID_ROLES locks variant names ( campaign_file.py:61 ) to {admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other} — not DRE substrategy names. Plus schedule_id is campaign-level. Forces per-sequence campaign files,
 not one file with 13 variants.

 3. Full copy file not read yet — only sampled sequences 1–3. Need to verify all 13 pass the validator (meeting link ≥1 step, codecombat.com/schools link ≥2 steps, ≥5-day intervals, merge fields present,
 banned-phrase clean).

 Given this, entering plan mode now before writing any code. Questions Steven must answer are embedded.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ⏺ I've now read all 13 sequences + Steven's own pre-activation checklist at the bottom (lines 1075-1087). Three blockers require Steven's explicit decision before I can write a plan:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shif +tab to cycle)

 1. Template 43784 — The copy says Step 2 of ALL 13 is **Template:** 43784 (existing Outreach template). tools.outreach_client.create_sequence has NO code path to reference an existing template — it creates a
 new template for every step. Three options differ in cost and cleanliness.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 2. codecombat.com/schools link requirement — The validator defaults require_cc_schools_link=True (needs the URL in ≥2 step bodies). Steven's copy has it in ZERO non-step-2 bodies (likely because template 43784
 covers it). Validation will hard-fail unless relaxed.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 3. Plain-text → body_html conversion — Copy is plain text with blank-line paragraph breaks. Outreach needs HTML ( <br><br> between paragraphs, <a> tags around URLs per copy rules line 54). I need to know how
 much of this pipeline exists vs. I need to build.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 Also: per-sequence schedule (52 Admin vs 53 Teacher) I can propose a mapping; Steven can override. Low-risk.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 do you want Step 2 handled?

 content into each seq │ Pros: │

 attach 43784 manually │ - Visible in validator │

 after │ │

 to accept existing │ - 13 duplicate templates in Outreach │

 template_id │ - If you edit 43784, clones don't update │

 │ - Template library bloat │

 ⏺ Update ( SCOUT_HISTORY.md )

 ⎿   Added 59 lines

 1583 ---

 1585 + ## Session 72 (2026-04-16) — DRE Family Collapsed 21→13 + Steven's claude.ai Copy Delivered (Read-Only Session)

 1586 +

 1587 + **Session theme:** Take S71's theoretical 21-sequence DRE framework and collapse it to a data-backed sequence set by running live classification passes over the SF Leads universe. Five classif

 + ication passes + two filter passes on 76,471 rows (measured) produced 13 locked sequences covering 26,137 eligible leads (measured). Session ended at context-budget ceiling with Steven's claud

 + e.ai-revised copy file landed in repo but not yet diffed / integrated / built — Phase 2 deferred cleanly to S73.

 1588 +

 1589 + ### 1. Five classification passes produced the 13-sequence lock

 1590 +

 1591 + - Pass 1–5 iterated on SF Leads role + grade + substrategy classification. The S71 theoretical matrix (TC 9 × LQD 9 × LIB 2 × UNI 1 = 21 sequences) did not survive contact with the real data —

 + several cells had <50 leads and collapsed into residuals.

 1592 + - Final lock: **13 sequences** with eligible cohorts (all measured, after active-account and 90-day no-touch filters):

 1593 + 1. INT-Universal 867 (ship first as safety net)

 1594 + 2. TC-Universal-Residual 6,957

 1595 + 3. TC-MS 5,452

 1596 + 4. TC-HS 4,892

 1597 + 5. TC-Elem 4,941

 1598 + 6. TC-Virtual 441

 1599 + 7. TC-District 442

 1600 + 8. TC-All-Grades 126

 1601 + 9. LIB 633

 1602 + 10. LQD-Universal 407

 1603 + 11. INT-Teacher 404

 1604 + 12. TC-Teacher 393

 1605 + 13. IT-ReEngage 182 (TC-IT + INT-IT merged)

 1606 + - TC substrategy dominates at **89% measured** of the DRE universe. Full derivation at `~/Desktop/DRE_Sequence_Family_Derivation_S72.md`.

 1607 + - Framework memory rewritten at `project_dre_family_framework.md` to reflect the locked 13 (replacing S71's 21).

 1608 +

 1609 + ### 2. Steven's claude.ai copy file landed — not yet integrated

 1610 +

 1611 + - File: `docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md` (40KB / 1,086 lines measured, copied from `~/Downloads/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy.md`).

 1612 + - Represents Steven's iteration pass on the S71 v2 base copy through claude.ai.

 1613 + - **Not diffed against S71 v2 base. Not integrated. Not validated against `feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md`.** Session 72 hit context-budget ceiling before Phase 2 work could start.

 1614 + - Phase 2 work = S73 first actions (listed in CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE).

 1615 +

 1616 + ### 3. Key finding that changes the add workflow

 1617 +

 1618 + - 99.83% measured of eligible DRE leads already exist in Outreach as prospects. S73 workflow is **`find_prospect_by_email` + verify state+tz + `add_prospect_to_sequence`** — NOT `create_prospe

 + ct`. Only 36 measured leads need creation.

 1619 + - Rule 17 is mostly verification-not-population for DRE.

 1620 +

 1621 + ### 4. New memory files + data artifacts

 1622 +

 1623 + - **Memory shipped:**

 1624 + - `project_dre_family_framework.md` (rewrite — 13-seq locked, build order, naming/tag conventions)

 1625 + - `project_uni_lead_source_whitelist.md` (new — 22 exact Lead Source strings qualifying for INT substrategy, case-insensitive exact match)

 1626 + - `project_dre_cold_outreach_parked_cohort.md` (new — ~48k measured parked bought-list leads for future cold session)

 1627 + - **Reusable artifacts at /tmp (preserve across sessions):**

 1628 + - `sf_leads_dre_pass5.py` (classification)

 1629 + - `sf_leads_dre_filters.py` + `sf_leads_dre_filters_part2.py` (filters)

 1630 + - `dre_outreach_touch_cache.json` (26,944 measured `touchedAt` entries — reuse to skip API calls)

 1631 + - **.env append:** `GOOGLE_SHEETS_SF_IMPORTS_ID=15p Smpfd SlgoaBFxbwqu UjtO9xYSnK-4yA69mkw_lWLk`

 1632 +

 1633 + ### 5. Exclusions and parks

 1634 +

 1635 + - **Do NOT touch:** 40 measured Code Ninjas rows (corporate deal, not channel), 433 measured homeschool individual-family rows (not customers).

 1636 + - **Parked for future cold-outreach session:** ~48,000 measured rows (Zen Prospect/Agile/MCH/Gov Spend/K12Prospects bought lists). These are legitimate in-territory targets but not dormant-re-en

 + gage appropriate. Reference: `project_dre_cold_outreach_parked_cohort.md`.

 1637 +

 1638 + ### 6. Repo state

 1639 +

 1640 + - S72 work committed + pushed end-of-session. Commits `f9cc849` (S72 EOS) + `eecc54a` (transcript). Working tree clean except `.DS_Store`.

 1641 +

 1642 + ---

 1643 +

 1644 ## Session 71 (2026-04-16) — Thursday Diocesan Drip Done + C4 Status Drift Cleaned + Sequence Coverage Mapped + DRE Family Framework Evolved

 1646 **Session theme:** Thursday 2026-04-16 diocesan drip executed + major audit of Queue status drift (triggered by Steven's "90% sure there's a mistake" on the 1,245+247 backlog numbers) + 24-str

 ategy sequence coverage mapped authoritatively + Strategy #12 Dormant Re-Engage framework evolved from one sequence into a 21-sequence family spanning 4 substrategies × role × grade. Dormant v

 2 draft shipped to Google Doc; Steven took to claude.ai for copy dial-in at EOS.

 22 - **Repo state:** S72 work committed + pushed end-of-session.

 24 - **End of Session 71, 2026-04-16 16:33 CDT — diocesan drip done, C4 status drift cleaned, DRE family framework:**

 25 -

 26 - - **Thursday 2026-04-16 diocesan drip — done.** 14 contacts loaded live (measured), 63/63 total plans `done`. Latent bug discovered: Steven's desktop-app attempt at 11:08 CDT had failed silently

 - with `sequence_not_enabled` because `/tmp/outreach_tokens.json` was missing (tmp cleared on reboot). Fix: copied `memory/outreach_tokens.json` → `/tmp/...`, force-refreshed, verified all 6 seqs

 - enabled, reset 14 `skipped` → `pending`, re-executed. Tracked as `project_diocesan_drip_silent_skip_on_missing_tmp_token.md`.

 Before I load 6 memory files and a 40KB copy file — quick context check. That's a substantial read ( ~25–35K tokens input). The current ctx: -- marker means the sidecar isn't feeding me a real number, so I

 can't self-gauge via the 40% rule. I want to do this right.

 Two paths — which do you want:

 (a) Full Phase 1 in this turn. I load all 6 memory files, read the full 40KB copy end-to-end, extract the shared voice, run the 13-sequence verification pass against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , and come

 back with: voice spec + pass/fail per sequence + entering plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers and INT-Universal. Likely burns significant ctx but is what the S73 first-actions block prescribes.

 (b) Staged. I start with the 6 memory files + skim the copy file's table-of-contents / sequence headers only, report what shape the file is actually in, then you decide whether to do the full voice extraction

 + verification in this turn or split. Lower ctx burn upfront, slower overall.

 My recommendation: (a) . The S73 actions were deliberately scoped by S72-me to fit in one session of focused work, and splitting the verification across turns risks losing the shared-voice context between

 sequences 1–7 and 8–13. But if you want to conserve budget, (b) is valid.

 26 - - **Thursday 2026-04-16 diocesan drip — done.** 14 contacts loaded live (measured), 63/63 total plans `done`. Latent bug discovered: Steven's desktop-app attempt at 11:08 CDT had failed silently

 - with `sequence_not_enabled` because `/tmp/outreach_tokens.json` was missing (tmp cleared on reboot). Fix: copied `memory/outreach_tokens.json` → `/tmp/...`, force-refreshed, verified all 6 seqs

 - enabled, reset 14 `skipped` → `pending`, re-executed. Tracked as `project_diocesan_drip_silent_skip_on_missing_tmp_token.md`.

 27 - - **C4 cold_license_request Queue status drift cleaned.** Starting state: 1,245 pending (measured). Steven's hunch "I'm 90% sure there's a mistake" validated: 1,092 rows (measured) were ALREADY

 - in C4 seqs 1995-1998 (S43 loaded them but Queue status was never back-written). Additional audit of the 150 "unmatched" via live Outreach: 7 newly loaded (measured), 86 already in OTHER sequence

 - s (measured), 46 opted-out (measured), 11 no-state (measured), 3 empty email. Result state: 1,099 complete / 143 skipped (w/ distinctive Notes markers for filtering) / 3 true pending. Scripts ra

 - n bulk sheet batchUpdate with 21,840 cells updated (measured) + 3,000 cells updated (measured) for classification cleanup.

 28 - - **24-strategy sequence coverage mapped accurately.** Prior CLAUDE.md framing "build sequences for the 23 strategies" was wrong — 18 of 24 have zero Scout-helped sequences. New authoritative me

 - mory: `project_sequence_coverage_s71.md`. 5 of 24 strategies + diocesan have Scout-helped sequences (#7, #9/#11 shared, #10, #13, #14). Steven confirmed line-by-line which seqs Scout helped vs h

 - e built solo.

 29 - - **Dormant Re-Engage Strategy #12 evolved to DRE family.** Not one sequence — **21 sequences** (4 substrategies × role × grade): TC (Teacher-Created) 9 variants, LQD (License/Quote/Demo — merge

 - d) 9 variants, LIB (Library/Librarian) 2 variants, UNI (Universal fallback) 1 variant. Full framework in `project_dre_family_framework.md`. Naming convention `DRE-<SUB>-<ROLE>-<GRADE>` + tag con

 - vention documented. Build order: UNI (safety net first), TC, LQD, LIB.

 30 - - **SF Leads role+state segmentation complete.** 76,468 leads in-territory (measured). Top buckets: admin 28,892 (measured — 37.78% measured), empty-TC 24,491 (measured — 32.03% measured), it 9,

 - 873 (measured). TX dominates at 25% of universe (measured). Full rollups in subagent output file; key insight: the empty-TC (teacher-created, empty title) cohort is THE dormant teacher pool for

 - Strategy #12 sub-A.

 31 - - **Sheet IDs now saved to memory.** `reference_scout_sheet_ids.md` documents Scout Master Sheet 1 (in .env), Scout SF Imports (NOT in .env — recommend adding), My Master Territory List (in .env

 - ). Steven explicitly flagged this as crucial after Scout couldn't answer "what's the sheet ID" on its own.

 - and Outreach info dump template 43784 at Step 2. All quality checks pass. Steven taking to claude.ai for copy dial-in; next session will receive revisions, then evolved framework says to build U

 - NI first as safety net.

 - tmp_token.md`, `feedback_chart_format_preference.md`.

 - filter fix), HIGH-9 (scripts `classify_role` → `_classify_role_c4` rename), HIGH-13 (bug5_phase0_scan fail-fast on missing columns), HIGH-11 (bug5_cleanup_lackland off-by-one + live-sheet purge

 41 - - **4 audit themes shipped** (commits `d5a872b`, `af9b96c`, `7a31658`, `31230f7`). Theme #1 `asyncio.get_event_loop()` → `asyncio.get_running_loop()` across 108 sites measured (100 in main.py +

 - 7 in research_engine.py + 1 in phase1e_executor_test.py; dead-branch in test_round1_unit.py left alone). Theme #2 `resp.raise_for_status()` added before 15 unprotected Serper `.json()` call site

 - s measured (13 in signal_processor.py + 1 in private_schools.py + 1 in compliance_gap_scanner.py). Theme #3 `chr(65+col)` base-26 overflow fix in 3 scripts (enrich_c4_pass2.py + phase1_ground_tr

 - uth_lackland.py + phase2b_sample_fingerprints.py), all 9 edge cases A→Z→AA→AZ→BA→ZZ→AAA verified. Theme #4 shared `scripts/_env.py::load_env_or_die` adopted by 5 active scripts (load_campaign.py

 - , f4_serper_replay.py, fetch_csta_roster.py, audit_c4_prospects.py, enrich_c4_pass2.py). ~26 remaining dead one-shot scripts not updated — pattern established for future adoption.

 42 - - **CRIT-2 verified live in production.** S69 committed the placeholder-token guard (commit `65976b9`) but the fix was a no-op until GAS redeploy. S70 walked Steven through the redeploy via a De

 - sktop `.gs.txt` file (`~/Desktop/scout_bridge_code.gs.txt`, Steven hand-edited line 22 to preserve the real token). `/ping_gas` via Telethon returned `✅ GAS Bridge connected! Running as: steven

 - @codecombat.com` — fix live.

 43 - - **S70 incident log.** (1) `fetch_csta_roster.py --help` accidentally rebuilt `memory/csta_roster.json` during Theme #4 verification — reverted via `git checkout HEAD --` before commit, S63 han

 - d-curation preserved. (2) `enrich_c4_pass2.py --help` **completed a full enrichment pipeline run in the background** after I thought I had killed it. My earlier "zero sheet writes" claim was wro

 - ng — the `| head -3` pipe closed stdout but the Python process kept running silently, reached `batchUpdate` at line 1056, and wrote **539 updates to the Prospecting Queue** (measured, from the s

 - tate file diff): 478 titles added, 12 parent districts added, 8 states added, 2 international flags. The script is idempotent + additive (only fills in missing fields, never overwrites existing

 - values), so the net effect is a legitimate C4 re-enrichment run — same pipeline Steven would run deliberately. The `scripts/c4_enrichment_pass2.json` state file was reverted in the EOS wrap (acc

 - idental run → don't commit the stats), but the sheet mutations are live. (3) R20 scanner false-fired on descriptive prose phrases ("set three column", "Remove the unused line") that matched dest

 - ructive-verb patterns without containing actual numbers — non-blocking, corrections emitted. Future cleanup: add argparse guards to the 3 top-level-execution scripts; see `memory/reference_scrip

 - ts_missing_argparse_guards.md`.

 55 - - **Rule 17 expanded (S66).** Both `state` AND IANA timezone are hard-required BEFORE `create_prospect` AND BEFORE `add_prospect_to_sequence`. Two failure modes this prevents: (1) mergefield ren

 - dering — Scout sequences use `{{state}}` in bodies, Outreach does NOT block missing mergefields, broken email ships; (2) schedule optimization — multi-window schedules (52 Admin, 53 Teacher) pic

 - k per-prospect local-time windows from timezone. Missing either = skip the contact, never fall back. Code-enforced by `tools.outreach_client.validate_prospect_inputs`.

 56 - - **S66 prioritization plan PARKED.** 6-tier budget-weighted plan across the 23 active strategies at `memory/project_prioritization_plan_s66.md`. 4,000-4,500 emails/week cap, July 31 goal refram

 - ed to Sep 30 (end of buying window). Revisit after S67 sequence-build work.

 57 - - **Session 67 focus (Steven's explicit direction at S66 EOS):** build email sequences for the 23 active prospecting strategies. Start with Tier 1 (Warm re-engage — strategies 9/10/11/12) since

 - those are highest-ROI and have the biggest backlog (1,245 cold_license_request + 247 winback March measured — per C4). In parallel, Steven is tackling usage-data fixes in Salesforce + CodeCombat

 - backend starting Wed 2026-04-15 (his work, not Scout's).

 58 - - **Repo state:** S66 wrap pushed to `origin/main`. Working tree clean except `.DS_Store`. S67 first work = this CLAUDE.md trim.

 Pivot: S73 DRE Phase 1. Per CLAUDE.md line 15, first actions are (1) load 6 memory files, (2) read docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md end-to-end, (3) extract shared base voice, (4) verify each of 13

 sequences against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , (5) enter plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers + INT-Universal build.

 Before I load 6 memory files and a 40KB copy file — quick context check. That's a substantial read ( ~25–35K tokens input). The current ctx: -- marker means the sidecar isn't feeding me a real number, so I

 can't self-gauge via the 40% rule. I want to do this right.

 Two paths — which do you want:

 (a) Full Phase 1 in this turn. I load all 6 memory files, read the full 40KB copy end-to-end, extract the shared voice, run the 13-sequence verification pass against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , and come

 back with: voice spec + pass/fail per sequence + entering plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers and INT-Universal. Likely burns significant ctx but is what the S73 first-actions block prescribes.

 (b) Staged. I start with the 6 memory files + skim the copy file's table-of-contents / sequence headers only, report what shape the file is actually in, then you decide whether to do the full voice extraction

 + verification in this turn or split. Lower ctx burn upfront, slower overall.

 My recommendation: (a) . The S73 actions were deliberately scoped by S72-me to fit in one session of focused work, and splitting the verification across turns risks losing the shared-voice context between

 sequences 1–7 and 8–13. But if you want to conserve budget, (b) is valid.

 are days apart, make sure formatting is right, make sure links are right then notify me to review them

 — comfortable.

 ⏺ Surveyed enough. Critical constraints found:

 1. create_sequence creates a new template for every step (outreach_client.py:1354-1371) — no path to reference an existing template_id . Step 2 = "template 43784" means I'd duplicate 43784's content into 13 new

 templates OR patch the code.

 2. VALID_ROLES locks variant names ( campaign_file.py:61 ) to {admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other} — not DRE substrategy names. Plus schedule_id is campaign-level. Forces per-sequence campaign files,

 not one file with 13 variants.

 3. Full copy file not read yet — only sampled sequences 1–3. Need to verify all 13 pass the validator (meeting link ≥1 step, codecombat.com/schools link ≥2 steps, ≥5-day intervals, merge fields present,

 banned-phrase clean).

 Given this, entering plan mode now before writing any code. Questions Steven must answer are embedded.

 ⏺ I've now read all 13 sequences + Steven's own pre-activation checklist at the bottom (lines 1075-1087). Three blockers require Steven's explicit decision before I can write a plan:

 1. Template 43784 — The copy says Step 2 of ALL 13 is **Template:** 43784 (existing Outreach template). tools.outreach_client.create_sequence has NO code path to reference an existing template — it creates a

 new template for every step. Three options differ in cost and cleanliness.

 2. codecombat.com/schools link requirement — The validator defaults require_cc_schools_link=True (needs the URL in ≥2 step bodies). Steven's copy has it in ZERO non-step-2 bodies (likely because template 43784

 covers it). Validation will hard-fail unless relaxed.

 3. Plain-text → body_html conversion — Copy is plain text with blank-line paragraph breaks. Outreach needs HTML ( <br><br> between paragraphs, <a> tags around URLs per copy rules line 54). I need to know how

 much of this pipeline exists vs. I need to build.

 Also: per-sequence schedule (52 Admin vs 53 Teacher) I can propose a mapping; Steven can override. Low-risk.

 ⏺ Trim committed ( a5bc138 ). Not pushing yet — will bundle with the DRE work at EOS, or push on your word.

 ---

 Pivot: S73 DRE Phase 1. Per CLAUDE.md line 15, first actions are (1) load 6 memory files, (2) read docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md end-to-end, (3) extract shared base voice, (4) verify each of 13

 sequences against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , (5) enter plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers + INT-Universal build.

 Before I load 6 memory files and a 40KB copy file — quick context check. That's a substantial read ( ~25–35K tokens input). The current ctx: -- marker means the sidecar isn't feeding me a real number, so I

 can't self-gauge via the 40% rule. I want to do this right.

 Two paths — which do you want:

 (a) Full Phase 1 in this turn. I load all 6 memory files, read the full 40KB copy end-to-end, extract the shared voice, run the 13-sequence verification pass against feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md , and come

 back with: voice spec + pass/fail per sequence + entering plan-mode for the 3 filter helpers and INT-Universal. Likely burns significant ctx but is what the S73 first-actions block prescribes.

 (b) Staged. I start with the 6 memory files + skim the copy file's table-of-contents / sequence headers only, report what shape the file is actually in, then you decide whether to do the full voice extraction

 + verification in this turn or split. Lower ctx burn upfront, slower overall.

 My recommendation: (a) . The S73 actions were deliberately scoped by S72-me to fit in one session of focused work, and splitting the verification across turns risks losing the shared-voice context between

 sequences 1–7 and 8–13. But if you want to conserve budget, (b) is valid.

 ❯ the sequence copy is already done. i have finished all 13 sequences in claude.ai and put them in that doc. no need to do anything to them other than upload them into outreach.io, tag them, make sure steps

 are days apart, make sure formatting is right, make sure links are right then notify me to review them

 ⏺ Got it — scope clarified. No copy work; just upload, tag, validate intervals, validate formatting, validate links, notify.

 This falls under PREFLIGHT: Campaign load + Outreach work. Loading required memory files + surveying the copy file + looking at scripts/load_campaign.py format in parallel before planning. Ctx headroom is 39%

 — comfortable.

 Searched for 3 patterns, read 5 files, listed 1 directory, recalled 3 memories (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  Loaded tools/CLAUDE.md

 ⏺ Surveyed enough. Critical constraints found:

 1. create_sequence creates a new template for every step (outreach_client.py:1354-1371) — no path to reference an existing template_id . Step 2 = "template 43784" means I'd duplicate 43784's content into 13 new

 templates OR patch the code.

 2. VALID_ROLES locks variant names ( campaign_file.py:61 ) to {admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other} — not DRE substrategy names. Plus schedule_id is campaign-level. Forces per-sequence campaign files,

 not one file with 13 variants.

 3. Full copy file not read yet — only sampled sequences 1–3. Need to verify all 13 pass the validator (meeting link ≥1 step, codecombat.com/schools link ≥2 steps, ≥5-day intervals, merge fields present,

 banned-phrase clean).

 Given this, entering plan mode now before writing any code. Questions Steven must answer are embedded.

 ⏺ Entered plan mode

 Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.

 ⏺ Already did substantial Phase 1 exploration in prior turns (read CLAUDE.md, DRE copy file top, DRE framework, copy rules, schedule map, campaign_file.py fully, create_sequence fully, canary_test.md,

 load_campaign.py top). Skipping Explore agents — I have what I need. Reading the rest of the copy file to spot-check validator compliance for all 13 sequences before committing to the plan.

 Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ I've now read all 13 sequences + Steven's own pre-activation checklist at the bottom (lines 1075-1087). Three blockers require Steven's explicit decision before I can write a plan:

 1. Template 43784 — The copy says Step 2 of ALL 13 is **Template:** 43784 (existing Outreach template). tools.outreach_client.create_sequence has NO code path to reference an existing template — it creates a

 new template for every step. Three options differ in cost and cleanliness.

 2. codecombat.com/schools link requirement — The validator defaults require_cc_schools_link=True (needs the URL in ≥2 step bodies). Steven's copy has it in ZERO non-step-2 bodies (likely because template 43784

 covers it). Validation will hard-fail unless relaxed.

 3. Plain-text → body_html conversion — Copy is plain text with blank-line paragraph breaks. Outreach needs HTML ( <br><br> between paragraphs, <a> tags around URLs per copy rules line 54). I need to know how

 much of this pipeline exists vs. I need to build.

 Also: per-sequence schedule (52 Admin vs 53 Teacher) I can propose a mapping; Steven can override. Low-risk.

 ⎿  Tool use rejected

 ⏺ You've got questions before picking. What would you like me to clarify? I can go deeper on any of the three — the template-43784 options, the cc-schools validator tradeoff, or the per-sequence schedule

 assignments — or there may be a fourth thing I didn't surface that you're thinking about.

 ❯ none of the other times you created sequences for me did we have any issue with you linking the template and not having to clone it.

 you can put codecombat.com/schools in step 6 of each template.

 yes you can do the html coonversion.

 Yes you can do the schedules as you think best.

 When its timeto add prospects you will need to make sure they have a state and time zone.

 ⏺ Understood on the last three. The first point is a correction I should verify in code, not argue back on. Checking for the template-reuse pattern now — if it exists I should be using it.

 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Plan: Upload 13 DRE Sequences to Outreach (S73)

 Context

 Session 72 locked the DRE sequence family at 13 sequences (26,137 measured eligible leads) and Steven's claude.ai-revised final copy landed in the repo at docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md . Session 73

 ships those 13 sequences into Outreach: copy is done, no further content work — just Outreach creation with correct intervals, tags, links, schedules, template-reuse for Step 2 (template 43784), and HTML

 conversion, then notify Steven to review and activate (Rule 15).

 Decisions from Steven (already resolved in-turn)

 1. Template 43784 reuse — The S43 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py shipped a working use_existing_template pattern that was never promoted to the library (Rule 18 debt). Resolution: promote that pattern to

 tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence via a per-step template_id field, then use it for DRE.

 2. codecombat.com/schools link — Append a codecombat.com/schools hyperlink line to Step 6 of each sequence at build time. Set require_cc_schools_link=False on the validator call since only 1 step body will

 contain it (template 43784 covers the canonical mention).

 3. HTML conversion — Authorized. Convert copy plain-text bodies (blank-line paragraph separators + raw URLs) to Outreach HTML ( <br><br> paragraph joins + <a href> URL wraps).

 4. Schedule mapping — Proposed mapping approved (see below).

 5. Em dashes — 26 occurrences in body text found via grep. Resolution: mechanical replace — → , (comma + space) at build time. Copy file stays untouched.

 6. Prospect-add (future session) — Every prospect must have state + IANA timezone before add_prospect_to_sequence (Rule 17). Not in S73 scope; S73 is create-only. Flagged for S74.

 Files to modify

 Library patches (promote S43 ephemeral pattern to library)

 - tools/outreach_client.py — 3 surgical edits:

 a. create_sequence (line 1198) — accept template_id: int key on each step dict. When present, skip POST /templates and use that id directly in the POST /sequenceTemplates link payload. Mirrors

 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py:136-187 .

 b. validate_sequence_inputs (line 814) — for steps with template_id only (no subject / body_html ), skip body banned-phrase check, em-dash check, cc-schools tally, meeting-link tally. The existing template is

 assumed validated on prior upload.

 c. verify_sequence (post-write) — existing code reads live state; no change needed, the linked-template path produces identical downstream state.

 - scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — add ≥2 unit tests:

 a. Step with only template_id passes validator (no spurious body-check failures).

 b. Mixed sequence (some template_id steps, some full steps) passes if full steps pass their own checks.

 New orchestrator script

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py (new, ~350-400 lines) — modeled directly on scripts/create_c4_sequences.py .

 - Responsibilities:

 - Parse docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md into 13 sequence dicts using the existing heading patterns ( ^# N\. <name> , ^## Step N — Interval X , ^\*\*Subject:\*\* , ^\*\*Template:\*\* ).

 - For each step body: run em-dash replace ( — → , ), append cc-schools line to Step 6 only, wrap URLs as <a href> , convert blank-line paragraphs to <br><br> . Pure-function helper body_text_to_html(text)

 defined in-script; tested inline.

 - Build a list of 13 {name, steps, schedule_id, tags, description} dicts using the schedule mapping above.

 - Per sequence, call create_sequence(..., require_cc_schools_link=False, meeting_link="https://hello.codecombat.com/c/steven/t/131", verify_after_create=True) .

 - On each step: {template_id: 43784} for Step 2; {subject, body_html, interval_seconds} for Steps 1/3/4/5/6.

 - Intervals: Step 1 = 300s (validator requires ≥1, ≤600). Steps 2-6 = 432000s (5 days). Copy says "Interval 0" for step 1 which the validator blocks; 300s is the conventional minimum.

 - Sequence tags: ["dre-2026-spring", "dre-<substrategy>"] e.g. ["dre-2026-spring", "dre-tc-ms"] . Per-prospect tags ( dre-state-<st> , dre-grade-<g> ) get applied at prospect-add time in S74, NOT at

 sequence-create.

 - Sequence name: "DRE 2026 Spring — <Cohort Name>" e.g. "DRE 2026 Spring — TC-MS" .

 - Sequence description: "Dormant Re-Engage 2026 Spring. Cohort: <name>, <size> eligible leads. See project_dre_family_framework.md." (no automation language — banned by validator).

 - Argparse: --dry-run (parse + build step dicts + call validate_sequence_inputs per sequence, no API POSTs), --create (full run), --only <N> (run one sequence by build-order index, useful for incremental

 rollout), --resume (read state sidecar and skip already-created sequences by name).

 - State sidecar: data/dre_2026_spring.state.json — tracks {sequence_name → sequence_id, created_at, steps: [...]} to support --resume across crashes.

 - Output: translate sequence_id → sequence name in all stdout (Rule 19); print a summary table at end with created sequence URLs ( https://app.outreach.io/sequences/<id> — URL OK per Rule 19, raw bare IDs

 not).

 No edits to locked copy file

 - docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md — NOT modified. Em-dash replace + cc-schools appends happen in memory at build time. The copy file is the frozen source-of-truth per Steven's direction.

 Build order (matches CLAUDE.md / DRE framework)

 INT-Universal ships FIRST as the safety net (smallest cohort, broadest fallback voice). Then TC-Universal-Residual (biggest cohort, locks TC voice). Then the TC grade splits. Then specialty variants. Per

 project_dre_family_framework.md line 21-27 and copy file line 13-27.

 Execution order in-session

 1. Patch tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence + validate_sequence_inputs for template_id support. Small, surgical. ≤30 lines of code change total.

 2. Patch scripts/test_outreach_validator.py with the 2 new tests. Run .venv/bin/python scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — must pass.

 3. Commit: feat(outreach): promote use_existing_template pattern from S43 C4 script to library (Rule 8 — one commit per feature).

 4. Write scripts/create_dre_sequences.py . Inline unit test for body_text_to_html runs at --dry-run entry.

 5. Run scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --dry-run . Confirm all 13 pass validate_sequence_inputs . Fix any surprises.

 6. Commit: feat(dre): orchestrator script for 13 sequence creates (Rule 8).

 7. Run scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --create . ~90 POST calls over ~5-10 minutes (65 new templates for Steps 1/3/4/5/6 × 13 sequences, 13 sequence creates, 78 sequence Step creates, 78 sequence Template

 links). Staggered by create_sequence 's built-in sleeps.

 8. Verify post-write state via verify_sequence return values (already automatic in create_sequence ).

 9. Commit: feat(dre): 13 DRE sequences live in Outreach (disabled, awaiting activation) with sequence-URL list in commit body.

 10. Push all 3 commits to origin/main.

 11. Notify Steven: 13 sequence names + Outreach URLs in a single message, with explicit Rule 15 callout that he manually activates each.

 Verification

 Pre-upload (dry-run):

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --dry-run — prints 13 sequence specs with per-step word counts, interval summary, schedule assignment, link-presence check (meeting link present ≥1, cc-schools appended to

 Step 6 of each).

 - .venv/bin/python scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — all unit tests green including 2 new template_id tests.

 Post-upload:

 - verify_sequence runs automatically after each create via create_sequence . A validation_failures list in the return means the live state doesn't match intent — fix or rollback ( DELETE /sequences/<id> ) before

 moving on.

 - Manual spot-check: open 2 sequences in Outreach UI (1 Teacher-schedule, 1 Admin-schedule): confirm Step 2 shows template 43784 (not a duplicate), meeting link renders as anchor tag on Step 5, cc-schools

 renders as anchor tag on Step 6, intervals show as 5 days for Steps 2-6.

 Steven-review checklist (delivered in the notify-Steven message):

 - 13 sequences created, all DISABLED (Rule 15).

 - Per-sequence: name, cohort size, schedule name, URL.

 - Known validator overrides applied: require_cc_schools_link=False (template 43784 covers it); step 1 interval = 300s not 0 (validator requires ≥1s).

 - Known copy mutation applied: em dashes replaced with , . Pointers to ~3 sample before/after for spot-check.

 - S74 scope note: prospect-add via find_prospect_by_email + add_prospect_to_sequence , NOT create_prospect (99.83% measured already exist in Outreach). Rule 17 (state + tz) enforced by validate_prospect_inputs .

 Rules compliance

 - Rule 1 (plan mode) — this plan.

 - Rule 4 (plans are one-shot, pressure-test) — 5 blocker questions surfaced and resolved before writing plan.

 - Rule 8 (one commit per feature) — 3 separate commits (library patch / orchestrator / live upload).

 - Rule 15 (all sequences are drafts; never auto-activate) — create_sequence creates DISABLED; Steven activates in UI.

 - Rule 17 (state + tz before prospect add) — out of scope for S73 create-only; noted for S74.

 - Rule 18 (promote ephemeral patterns) — THE key compliance move here: S43's use_existing_template goes from one-shot script to library.

 - Rule 19 (no raw Outreach IDs in chat) — orchestrator script translates ids → names + URLs in stdout.

 - Rule 20 (every number labeled) — cohort-size citations in commit messages + Steven-review message will carry measured labels.

 - Rule 21 (verify before instructing) — any S74 destructive instruction (e.g., "delete prospects X/Y/Z from sequence") will be preceded by a find_prospect_by_email or get_sequence_states call in the same turn.

 Rollback plan

 If a dry-run or partial --create reveals a blocking issue:

 - For a partial create (say, 4 of 13 landed before failure): DELETE the 4 landed sequences via _api_delete('/sequences/<id>') to get back to a clean slate before re-running. State sidecar tracks which 4.

 - For the library patch causing validator regression: git revert <commit> on the outreach_client.py patch and re-run unit tests. DRE work unblocked once patch is re-done cleanly.

 - For HTML conversion errors: revert the one commit with the orchestrator script; copy file is untouched; re-work the converter and re-run.

 ⏺ Important new finding before I finalize: 26 em-dash occurrences in body text of the copy file. validate_sequence_inputs line 913 hard-fails em dashes in bodies (every step, every sequence). Your own rule says

 the same ( feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md:13 ), but the claude.ai revision reintroduced them.

 One more decision needed before I can write the plan:

 ⏺  User answered Claude's questions:

 ⎿  · The DRE copy has 26 em-dash characters in body text (—). The validator hard-fails these and your own copy rules forbid them. How do you want them handled? → Mechanical replace: em dash → ", "

 ⏺ All 5 decisions resolved. Writing the plan file.

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan
 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/glimmering-singing-otter.md · /plan to edit
 Plan: Upload 13 DRE Sequences to Outreach (S73)

 Context

 Session 72 locked the DRE sequence family at 13 sequences (26,137 measured eligible leads) and Steven's claude.ai-revised final copy landed in the repo at docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md . Session 73

 ships those 13 sequences into Outreach: copy is done, no further content work — just Outreach creation with correct intervals, tags, links, schedules, template-reuse for Step 2 (template 43784), and HTML

 conversion, then notify Steven to review and activate (Rule 15).

 Decisions from Steven (already resolved in-turn)

 1. Template 43784 reuse — The S43 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py shipped a working use_existing_template pattern that was never promoted to the library (Rule 18 debt). Resolution: promote that pattern to

 tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence via a per-step template_id field, then use it for DRE.

 2. codecombat.com/schools link — Append a codecombat.com/schools hyperlink line to Step 6 of each sequence at build time. Set require_cc_schools_link=False on the validator call since only 1 step body will

 contain it (template 43784 covers the canonical mention).

 3. HTML conversion — Authorized. Convert copy plain-text bodies (blank-line paragraph separators + raw URLs) to Outreach HTML ( <br><br> paragraph joins + <a href> URL wraps).

 4. Schedule mapping — Proposed mapping approved (see below).

 5. Em dashes — 26 occurrences in body text found via grep. Resolution: mechanical replace — → , (comma + space) at build time. Copy file stays untouched.

 6. Prospect-add (future session) — Every prospect must have state + IANA timezone before add_prospect_to_sequence (Rule 17). Not in S73 scope; S73 is create-only. Flagged for S74.

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 4 │ TC-HS (4,892) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 5 │ TC-Elem (4,941) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 6 │ TC-Virtual (441) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 7 │ TC-District (442) │ 52 Admin Mon-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 8 │ TC-All-Grades (126) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 9 │ LIB (633) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 Files to modify

 Library patches (promote S43 ephemeral pattern to library)

 - tools/outreach_client.py — 3 surgical edits:

 a. create_sequence (line 1198) — accept template_id: int key on each step dict. When present, skip POST /templates and use that id directly in the POST /sequenceTemplates link payload. Mirrors

 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py:136-187 .

 b. validate_sequence_inputs (line 814) — for steps with template_id only (no subject / body_html ), skip body banned-phrase check, em-dash check, cc-schools tally, meeting-link tally. The existing template

 is assumed validated on prior upload.

 c. verify_sequence (post-write) — existing code reads live state; no change needed, the linked-template path produces identical downstream state.

 - scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — add ≥2 unit tests:

 a. Step with only template_id passes validator (no spurious body-check failures).

 b. Mixed sequence (some template_id steps, some full steps) passes if full steps pass their own checks.

 New orchestrator script

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py (new, ~350-400 lines) — modeled directly on scripts/create_c4_sequences.py .

 - Responsibilities:

 - Parse docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md into 13 sequence dicts using the existing heading patterns ( ^# N\. <name> , ^## Step N — Interval X , ^\*\*Subject:\*\* , ^\*\*Template:\*\* ).

 - For each step body: run em-dash replace ( — → , ), append cc-schools line to Step 6 only, wrap URLs as <a href> , convert blank-line paragraphs to <br><br> . Pure-function helper body_text_to_html(text)

 defined in-script; tested inline.

 - Build a list of 13 {name, steps, schedule_id, tags, description} dicts using the schedule mapping above.

 - Per sequence, call create_sequence(..., require_cc_schools_link=False, meeting_link="https://hello.codecombat.com/c/steven/t/131", verify_after_create=True) .

 - On each step: {template_id: 43784} for Step 2; {subject, body_html, interval_seconds} for Steps 1/3/4/5/6.

 - Intervals: Step 1 = 300s (validator requires ≥1, ≤600). Steps 2-6 = 432000s (5 days). Copy says "Interval 0" for step 1 which the validator blocks; 300s is the conventional minimum.

 - Sequence tags: ["dre-2026-spring", "dre-<substrategy>"] e.g. ["dre-2026-spring", "dre-tc-ms"] . Per-prospect tags ( dre-state-<st> , dre-grade-<g> ) get applied at prospect-add time in S74, NOT at

 sequence-create.

 - Sequence name: "DRE 2026 Spring — <Cohort Name>" e.g. "DRE 2026 Spring — TC-MS" .

 - Sequence description: "Dormant Re-Engage 2026 Spring. Cohort: <name>, <size> eligible leads. See project_dre_family_framework.md." (no automation language — banned by validator).

 - Argparse: --dry-run (parse + build step dicts + call validate_sequence_inputs per sequence, no API POSTs), --create (full run), --only <N> (run one sequence by build-order index, useful for incremental

 rollout), --resume (read state sidecar and skip already-created sequences by name).

 - State sidecar: data/dre_2026_spring.state.json — tracks {sequence_name → sequence_id, created_at, steps: [...]} to support --resume across crashes.

 - Output: translate sequence_id → sequence name in all stdout (Rule 19); print a summary table at end with created sequence URLs ( https://app.outreach.io/sequences/<id> — URL OK per Rule 19, raw bare IDs

 not).

 No edits to locked copy file

 - docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md — NOT modified. Em-dash replace + cc-schools appends happen in memory at build time. The copy file is the frozen source-of-truth per Steven's direction.

 Build order (matches CLAUDE.md / DRE framework)

 INT-Universal ships FIRST as the safety net (smallest cohort, broadest fallback voice). Then TC-Universal-Residual (biggest cohort, locks TC voice). Then the TC grade splits. Then specialty variants. Per

 project_dre_family_framework.md line 21-27 and copy file line 13-27.

 Execution order in-session

 1. Patch tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence + validate_sequence_inputs for template_id support. Small, surgical. ≤30 lines of code change total.

 2. Patch scripts/test_outreach_validator.py with the 2 new tests. Run .venv/bin/python scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — must pass.

 3. Commit: feat(outreach): promote use_existing_template pattern from S43 C4 script to library (Rule 8 — one commit per feature).

 4. Write scripts/create_dre_sequences.py . Inline unit test for body_text_to_html runs at --dry-run entry.

 5. Run scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --dry-run . Confirm all 13 pass validate_sequence_inputs . Fix any surprises.

 6. Commit: feat(dre): orchestrator script for 13 sequence creates (Rule 8).

 7. Run scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --create . ~90 POST calls over ~5-10 minutes (65 new templates for Steps 1/3/4/5/6 × 13 sequences, 13 sequence creates, 78 sequence Step creates, 78 sequence Template

 links). Staggered by create_sequence 's built-in sleeps.

 8. Verify post-write state via verify_sequence return values (already automatic in create_sequence ).

 9. Commit: feat(dre): 13 DRE sequences live in Outreach (disabled, awaiting activation) with sequence-URL list in commit body.

 10. Push all 3 commits to origin/main.

 11. Notify Steven: 13 sequence names + Outreach URLs in a single message, with explicit Rule 15 callout that he manually activates each.

 Verification

 Pre-upload (dry-run):

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --dry-run — prints 13 sequence specs with per-step word counts, interval summary, schedule assignment, link-presence check (meeting link present ≥1, cc-schools appended to

 Step 6 of each).

 - .venv/bin/python scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — all unit tests green including 2 new template_id tests.

 Post-upload:

 - verify_sequence runs automatically after each create via create_sequence . A validation_failures list in the return means the live state doesn't match intent — fix or rollback ( DELETE /sequences/<id> )

 before moving on.

 - Manual spot-check: open 2 sequences in Outreach UI (1 Teacher-schedule, 1 Admin-schedule): confirm Step 2 shows template 43784 (not a duplicate), meeting link renders as anchor tag on Step 5, cc-schools

 renders as anchor tag on Step 6, intervals show as 5 days for Steps 2-6.

 Steven-review checklist (delivered in the notify-Steven message):

 - 13 sequences created, all DISABLED (Rule 15).

 - Per-sequence: name, cohort size, schedule name, URL.

 - Known validator overrides applied: require_cc_schools_link=False (template 43784 covers it); step 1 interval = 300s not 0 (validator requires ≥1s).

 - Known copy mutation applied: em dashes replaced with , . Pointers to ~3 sample before/after for spot-check.

 - S74 scope note: prospect-add via find_prospect_by_email + add_prospect_to_sequence , NOT create_prospect (99.83% measured already exist in Outreach). Rule 17 (state + tz) enforced by

 validate_prospect_inputs .

 Rules compliance

 - Rule 1 (plan mode) — this plan.

 - Rule 4 (plans are one-shot, pressure-test) — 5 blocker questions surfaced and resolved before writing plan.

 - Rule 8 (one commit per feature) — 3 separate commits (library patch / orchestrator / live upload).

 - Rule 15 (all sequences are drafts; never auto-activate) — create_sequence creates DISABLED; Steven activates in UI.

 - Rule 17 (state + tz before prospect add) — out of scope for S73 create-only; noted for S74.

 - Rule 18 (promote ephemeral patterns) — THE key compliance move here: S43's use_existing_template goes from one-shot script to library.

 - Rule 19 (no raw Outreach IDs in chat) — orchestrator script translates ids → names + URLs in stdout.

 - Rule 20 (every number labeled) — cohort-size citations in commit messages + Steven-review message will carry measured labels.

 - Rule 21 (verify before instructing) — any S74 destructive instruction (e.g., "delete prospects X/Y/Z from sequence") will be preceded by a find_prospect_by_email or get_sequence_states call in the same

 turn.

 Rollback plan

 If a dry-run or partial --create reveals a blocking issue:

 - For a partial create (say, 4 of 13 landed before failure): DELETE the 4 landed sequences via _api_delete('/sequences/<id>') to get back to a clean slate before re-running. State sidecar tracks which 4.

 - For the library patch causing validator regression: git revert <commit> on the outreach_client.py patch and re-run unit tests. DRE work unblocked once patch is re-done cleanly.

 - For HTML conversion errors: revert the one commit with the orchestrator script; copy file is untouched; re-work the converter and re-run.

 ✢ 4 0s · ↑ 4.2k tokens)

 ⏺ Auto mode lets Claude handle permission prompts automatically — Claude checks each tool call for risky actions and prompt injection before executing. Actions Claude identifies as safe are executed,
 while actions Claude identifies as risky are blocked and Claude may try a different approach. Ideal for long-running tasks. Sessions are slightly more expensive. Claude can make mistakes that allow
 harmful commands to run, it's recommended to only use in isolated environments. Shift+Tab to change mode.

 ❯ the sequence copy is already done. i have finished all 13 sequences in claude.ai and put them in that doc. no need to do anything to them other than upload them into outreach.io, tag them, make sure steps

 are days apart, make sure formatting is right, make sure links are right then notify me to review them

 ⏺ Got it — scope clarified. No copy work; just upload, tag, validate intervals, validate formatting, validate links, notify.

 This falls under PREFLIGHT: Campaign load + Outreach work. Loading required memory files + surveying the copy file + looking at scripts/load_campaign.py format in parallel before planning. Ctx headroom is 39%

 — comfortable.

 Searched for 3 patterns, read 5 files, listed 1 directory, recalled 3 memories (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿  Loaded tools/CLAUDE.md

 ⏺ Surveyed enough. Critical constraints found:

 1. create_sequence creates a new template for every step (outreach_client.py:1354-1371) — no path to reference an existing template_id . Step 2 = "template 43784" means I'd duplicate 43784's content into 13 new

 templates OR patch the code.

 2. VALID_ROLES locks variant names ( campaign_file.py:61 ) to {admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other} — not DRE substrategy names. Plus schedule_id is campaign-level. Forces per-sequence campaign files,

 not one file with 13 variants.

 3. Full copy file not read yet — only sampled sequences 1–3. Need to verify all 13 pass the validator (meeting link ≥1 step, codecombat.com/schools link ≥2 steps, ≥5-day intervals, merge fields present,

 banned-phrase clean).

 Given this, entering plan mode now before writing any code. Questions Steven must answer are embedded.

 new template for every step. Three options differ in cost and cleanliness.

 covers it). Validation will hard-fail unless relaxed.

 much of this pipeline exists vs. I need to build.

 Plan: Upload 13 DRE Sequences to Outreach (S73)

 Context

 Session 72 locked the DRE sequence family at 13 sequences (26,137 measured eligible leads) and Steven's claude.ai-revised final copy landed in the repo at docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md . Session 73

 ships those 13 sequences into Outreach: copy is done, no further content work — just Outreach creation with correct intervals, tags, links, schedules, template-reuse for Step 2 (template 43784), and HTML

 conversion, then notify Steven to review and activate (Rule 15).

 Decisions from Steven (already resolved in-turn)

 1. Template 43784 reuse — The S43 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py shipped a working use_existing_template pattern that was never promoted to the library (Rule 18 debt). Resolution: promote that pattern to

 tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence via a per-step template_id field, then use it for DRE.

 2. codecombat.com/schools link — Append a codecombat.com/schools hyperlink line to Step 6 of each sequence at build time. Set require_cc_schools_link=False on the validator call since only 1 step body will

 contain it (template 43784 covers the canonical mention).

 3. HTML conversion — Authorized. Convert copy plain-text bodies (blank-line paragraph separators + raw URLs) to Outreach HTML ( <br><br> paragraph joins + <a href> URL wraps).

 4. Schedule mapping — Proposed mapping approved (see below).

 5. Em dashes — 26 occurrences in body text found via grep. Resolution: mechanical replace — → , (comma + space) at build time. Copy file stays untouched.

 6. Prospect-add (future session) — Every prospect must have state + IANA timezone before add_prospect_to_sequence (Rule 17). Not in S73 scope; S73 is create-only. Flagged for S74.

 ┌─────┬───────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┐

 │ Seq │ Cohort │ Schedule │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 4 │ TC-HS (4,892) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 5 │ TC-Elem (4,941) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 6 │ TC-Virtual (441) │ 53 Teacher Tue-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 7 │ TC-District (442) │ 52 Admin Mon-Thu │

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 ├─────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

 │ 13 │ IT-ReEngage (182) │ 52 Admin Mon-Thu │

 Files to modify

 Library patches (promote S43 ephemeral pattern to library)

 - tools/outreach_client.py — 3 surgical edits:

 a. create_sequence (line 1198) — accept template_id: int key on each step dict. When present, skip POST /templates and use that id directly in the POST /sequenceTemplates link payload. Mirrors

 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py:136-187 .

 b. validate_sequence_inputs (line 814) — for steps with template_id only (no subject / body_html ), skip body banned-phrase check, em-dash check, cc-schools tally, meeting-link tally. The existing template

 is assumed validated on prior upload.

 c. verify_sequence (post-write) — existing code reads live state; no change needed, the linked-template path produces identical downstream state.

 - scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — add ≥2 unit tests:

 a. Step with only template_id passes validator (no spurious body-check failures).

 b. Mixed sequence (some template_id steps, some full steps) passes if full steps pass their own checks.

 New orchestrator script

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py (new, ~350-400 lines) — modeled directly on scripts/create_c4_sequences.py .

 - Responsibilities:

 - Parse docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md into 13 sequence dicts using the existing heading patterns ( ^# N\. <name> , ^## Step N — Interval X , ^\*\*Subject:\*\* , ^\*\*Template:\*\* ).

 - For each step body: run em-dash replace ( — → , ), append cc-schools line to Step 6 only, wrap URLs as <a href> , convert blank-line paragraphs to <br><br> . Pure-function helper body_text_to_html(text)

 defined in-script; tested inline.

 - Build a list of 13 {name, steps, schedule_id, tags, description} dicts using the schedule mapping above.

 - Per sequence, call create_sequence(..., require_cc_schools_link=False, meeting_link="https://hello.codecombat.com/c/steven/t/131", verify_after_create=True) .

 - On each step: {template_id: 43784} for Step 2; {subject, body_html, interval_seconds} for Steps 1/3/4/5/6.

 - Intervals: Step 1 = 300s (validator requires ≥1, ≤600). Steps 2-6 = 432000s (5 days). Copy says "Interval 0" for step 1 which the validator blocks; 300s is the conventional minimum.

 - Sequence tags: ["dre-2026-spring", "dre-<substrategy>"] e.g. ["dre-2026-spring", "dre-tc-ms"] . Per-prospect tags ( dre-state-<st> , dre-grade-<g> ) get applied at prospect-add time in S74, NOT at

 sequence-create.

 - Sequence name: "DRE 2026 Spring — <Cohort Name>" e.g. "DRE 2026 Spring — TC-MS" .

 - Sequence description: "Dormant Re-Engage 2026 Spring. Cohort: <name>, <size> eligible leads. See project_dre_family_framework.md." (no automation language — banned by validator).

 - Argparse: --dry-run (parse + build step dicts + call validate_sequence_inputs per sequence, no API POSTs), --create (full run), --only <N> (run one sequence by build-order index, useful for incremental

 rollout), --resume (read state sidecar and skip already-created sequences by name).

 - State sidecar: data/dre_2026_spring.state.json — tracks {sequence_name → sequence_id, created_at, steps: [...]} to support --resume across crashes.

 - Output: translate sequence_id → sequence name in all stdout (Rule 19); print a summary table at end with created sequence URLs ( https://app.outreach.io/sequences/<id> — URL OK per Rule 19, raw bare IDs

 not).

 No edits to locked copy file

 - docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md — NOT modified. Em-dash replace + cc-schools appends happen in memory at build time. The copy file is the frozen source-of-truth per Steven's direction.

 Build order (matches CLAUDE.md / DRE framework)

 INT-Universal ships FIRST as the safety net (smallest cohort, broadest fallback voice). Then TC-Universal-Residual (biggest cohort, locks TC voice). Then the TC grade splits. Then specialty variants. Per

 project_dre_family_framework.md line 21-27 and copy file line 13-27.

 Execution order in-session

 1. Patch tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence + validate_sequence_inputs for template_id support. Small, surgical. ≤30 lines of code change total.

 2. Patch scripts/test_outreach_validator.py with the 2 new tests. Run .venv/bin/python scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — must pass.

 3. Commit: feat(outreach): promote use_existing_template pattern from S43 C4 script to library (Rule 8 — one commit per feature).

 4. Write scripts/create_dre_sequences.py . Inline unit test for body_text_to_html runs at --dry-run entry.

 5. Run scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --dry-run . Confirm all 13 pass validate_sequence_inputs . Fix any surprises.

 6. Commit: feat(dre): orchestrator script for 13 sequence creates (Rule 8).

 7. Run scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --create . ~90 POST calls over ~5-10 minutes (65 new templates for Steps 1/3/4/5/6 × 13 sequences, 13 sequence creates, 78 sequence Step creates, 78 sequence Template

 links). Staggered by create_sequence 's built-in sleeps.

 8. Verify post-write state via verify_sequence return values (already automatic in create_sequence ).

 9. Commit: feat(dre): 13 DRE sequences live in Outreach (disabled, awaiting activation) with sequence-URL list in commit body.

 10. Push all 3 commits to origin/main.

 11. Notify Steven: 13 sequence names + Outreach URLs in a single message, with explicit Rule 15 callout that he manually activates each.

 Verification

 Pre-upload (dry-run):

 - scripts/create_dre_sequences.py --dry-run — prints 13 sequence specs with per-step word counts, interval summary, schedule assignment, link-presence check (meeting link present ≥1, cc-schools appended to

 Step 6 of each).

 - .venv/bin/python scripts/test_outreach_validator.py — all unit tests green including 2 new template_id tests.

 Post-upload:

 - verify_sequence runs automatically after each create via create_sequence . A validation_failures list in the return means the live state doesn't match intent — fix or rollback ( DELETE /sequences/<id> )

 before moving on.

 - Manual spot-check: open 2 sequences in Outreach UI (1 Teacher-schedule, 1 Admin-schedule): confirm Step 2 shows template 43784 (not a duplicate), meeting link renders as anchor tag on Step 5, cc-schools

 renders as anchor tag on Step 6, intervals show as 5 days for Steps 2-6.

 Steven-review checklist (delivered in the notify-Steven message):

 - 13 sequences created, all DISABLED (Rule 15).

 - Per-sequence: name, cohort size, schedule name, URL.

 - Known validator overrides applied: require_cc_schools_link=False (template 43784 covers it); step 1 interval = 300s not 0 (validator requires ≥1s).

 - Known copy mutation applied: em dashes replaced with , . Pointers to ~3 sample before/after for spot-check.

 - S74 scope note: prospect-add via find_prospect_by_email + add_prospect_to_sequence , NOT create_prospect (99.83% measured already exist in Outreach). Rule 17 (state + tz) enforced by

 validate_prospect_inputs .

 Rules compliance

 - Rule 1 (plan mode) — this plan.

 - Rule 4 (plans are one-shot, pressure-test) — 5 blocker questions surfaced and resolved before writing plan.

 - Rule 8 (one commit per feature) — 3 separate commits (library patch / orchestrator / live upload).

 - Rule 15 (all sequences are drafts; never auto-activate) — create_sequence creates DISABLED; Steven activates in UI.

 - Rule 17 (state + tz before prospect add) — out of scope for S73 create-only; noted for S74.

 - Rule 18 (promote ephemeral patterns) — THE key compliance move here: S43's use_existing_template goes from one-shot script to library.

 - Rule 19 (no raw Outreach IDs in chat) — orchestrator script translates ids → names + URLs in stdout.

 - Rule 20 (every number labeled) — cohort-size citations in commit messages + Steven-review message will carry measured labels.

 - Rule 21 (verify before instructing) — any S74 destructive instruction (e.g., "delete prospects X/Y/Z from sequence") will be preceded by a find_prospect_by_email or get_sequence_states call in the same

 turn.

 Rollback plan

 If a dry-run or partial --create reveals a blocking issue:

 - For a partial create (say, 4 of 13 landed before failure): DELETE the 4 landed sequences via _api_delete('/sequences/<id>') to get back to a clean slate before re-running. State sidecar tracks which 4.

 - For the library patch causing validator regression: git revert <commit> on the outreach_client.py patch and re-run unit tests. DRE work unblocked once patch is re-done cleanly.

 - For HTML conversion errors: revert the one commit with the orchestrator script; copy file is untouched; re-work the converter and re-run.

 ⏺ Auto mode lets Claude handle permission prompts automatically — Claude checks each tool call for risky actions and prompt injection before executing. Actions Claude identifies as safe are executed,

 while actions Claude identifies as risky are blocked and Claude may try a different approach. Ideal for long-running tasks. Sessions are slightly more expensive. Claude can make mistakes that allow

 harmful commands to run, it's recommended to only use in isolated environments. Shift+Tab to change mode.

 ⏺ Plan approved. Executing.

 1. create_sequence creates a new template for every step (outreach_client.py:1354-1371) — no path to reference an existing template_id . Step 2 = "template 43784" means I'd duplicate 43784's content into 13 new

 templates OR patch the code.

 2. VALID_ROLES locks variant names ( campaign_file.py:61 ) to {admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other} — not DRE substrategy names. Plus schedule_id is campaign-level. Forces per-sequence campaign files,

 not one file with 13 variants.

 3. Full copy file not read yet — only sampled sequences 1–3. Need to verify all 13 pass the validator (meeting link ≥1 step, codecombat.com/schools link ≥2 steps, ≥5-day intervals, merge fields present,

 banned-phrase clean).

 Plan: Upload 13 DRE Sequences to Outreach (S73)

 Context

 Session 72 locked the DRE sequence family at 13 sequences (26,137 measured eligible leads) and Steven's claude.ai-revised final copy landed in the repo at docs/DRE_13_Sequences_Final_Copy_S72.md . Session 73

 ships those 13 sequences into Outreach: copy is done, no further content work — just Outreach creation with correct intervals, tags, links, schedules, template-reuse for Step 2 (template 43784), and HTML

 conversion, then notify Steven to review and activate (Rule 15).

 Decisions from Steven (already resolved in-turn)

 1. Template 43784 reuse — The S43 scripts/create_c4_sequences.py shipped a working use_existing_template pattern that was never promoted to the library (Rule 18 debt). Resolution: promote that pattern to

 tools/outreach_client.py::create_sequence via a per-step template_id field, then use it for DRE.

 2. codecombat.com/schools link — Append a codecombat.com/schools hyperlink line to Step 6 of each sequence at build time. Set require_cc_schools_link=False on the validator call since only 1 step body will

 contain it (template 43784 covers the canonical mention).

 3. HTML conversion — Authorized. Convert copy plain-text bodies (blank-line paragraph separators + raw URLs) to Outreach HTML ( <br><br> paragraph joins + <a href> URL wraps).

 4. Schedule mapping — Proposed mapping approved (see below).

 5. Em dashes — 26 occurrences in body text found via grep. Resolution: mechanical replace — → , (comma + space) at build time. Copy file stays untouched.

 6. Prospect-add (future session) — Every prospect must have state + IANA timezone before add_prospect_to_sequence (Rule 17). Not in S73 scope; S73 is create-only. Flagged for S74.
